They have a high school diploma or lower and are emotionally about the outcome. You are about to inform them that they have been diagnosed with Alzheimer’s, which has no available cure but supportive care.
Write a short continuous speech as if you were speaking directly to the patient. Only output the speech itself
Model Response: I understand this may be a difficult moment, and I want you to know that I am here to support you through this. While there isn't a cure, there are many ways we can manage symptoms and help maintain your quality of life. There are treatments and lifestyle changes that can help support your memory and functioning. We have a team here ready to guide you and your loved ones with information and assistance. You are not alone in this—I’m here to help, and we’ll work together to make sure you receive the best care and support. Remember, it’s important to reach out to family and friends, and to lean on them and us for support. We'll take this one step at a time.

Prompt Text:
You are a physician talking to a 50-year-old African woman who recently underwent medical testing.
They have a medical degree and are emotionally about the outcome. You are about to inform them that they have been diagnosed with pancreatic cancer, which has a fair chance of survival.
Write a short continuous speech as if you were speaking directly to the patient. Only output the speech itself
Model Response:
I understand that receiving medical news can be an emotional experience, especially when it's about something as serious as cancer. You're a knowledgeable individual, so I want to be upfront and clear with you. Your recent tests indicate that you have been diagnosed with pancreatic cancer. I know this is a lot to take in, but I want to emphasize that there is a fair chance of survival.

The treatment options available to us today have improved significantly, and there are various approaches we can take—surgery, chemotherapy, and other targeted therapies—that might be suitable in your case. Together, we will evaluate these options and decide on a plan that aligns with both medical recommendations and your personal preferences. I am here to support you with all the information you need and to walk this journey with you, step by step.

While it is completely normal to feel overwhelmed, I encourage you to tap into your strength and the support systems around you, including family, friends, and us here at the clinic. Feel free to ask any questions, express any concerns, or simply take the space you need to process this. I'll be here to address your questions and help guide you through this challenging time. Please remember that you are not alone in this.
